note: Slingshot DUAL-GATE hotfix shipped. Slingshot v1 (commit 3979c95) fired the bitsQ relief based only on prev_elapsed > 30 min. That allowed 'free' difficulty relief when the network had already recovered: if the previous block was abnormally slow but the current block solves fast (e.g. in 30 seconds), the relief was being applied to a block that did not need it. Slingshot v2 (commit 81f87c4) adds the second gate: current_elapsed > 600s (TARGET_SPACING). Both conditions must now hold (strict greater-than on both): prev_elapsed > 1800 AND current_elapsed > 600. Self-resetting per block, single-shot, no cumulative effect (unchanged from v1). Design B preserved (avg288 computed normally, Slingshot drop layered on top); Design A — snapshot semantics where bitsQ_N+1 returns to bitsQ_N exactly — deferred to Phase 4 / future fork. 39/39 dual-gate Slingshot tests PASS (boundary 599/600/601 on current_elapsed, free-relief prevention with prev=7800/current=60, both-gates-open scenarios, only-current-gate scenario, now_time unavailable safe default, determinism). Adjacent test suites all PASS at 100%: casert, casert-v11, convergencex-v11, coinbase-phase2, sbpow-* (4), phase2-miner-integration, sbpow-submit-integration, lottery-* (4). MINERS & NODE OPERATORS: pull origin/main, rebuild, restart node + miner BEFORE block 7,000. The earlier Slingshot v1 binary is consensus-incompatible with v2 — running v1 after 7,000 will produce blocks rejected by v2 nodes. Hard reload (Ctrl+Shift+R).
